action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|...B.q...|
 2|..##p...S|
 3|.....#.x.|
 4|....2#...|
 5|....y....|
 6|...#..#..|
 7|.........|
 8|.....#...|
 9|..#.1A..#|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (9,2) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (9,2), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (6,9): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {1, 2}. So it is open at t=1,2,5,6,... and closed at t=0,3,4,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (4,1): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {1, 2}. So it is open at t=1,2,5,6,... and closed at t=0,3,4,7,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (5,2); t mod 8=1: (5,1); t mod 8=2: (6,1); t mod 8=3: (6,2); t mod 8=4: (7,2); t mod 8=5: (6,2); t mod 8=6: (6,1); t mod 8=7: (5,1). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (6,1); t mod 4=1: (6,2); t mod 4=2: (5,2); t mod 4=3: (6,2). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (5,9) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (8,3) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (5,4) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (5,5) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 40 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 8 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I start at (9,2). I need to pick up parcel 1 at (5,9) and deliver it to (8,3), and parcel 2 at (5,4) and deliver it to (5,5). I'll first head to parcel 1's pickup at (5,9). From (9,2), I can go W to (5,2) then N to (5,9). But I must avoid guards. At t=0, guard P is at (5,2) and guard Q is at (6,1). If I move W to (5,2) at t=1, I'll be on P's cell at t=1 (since P moves to (5,1) at t=1, not (5,2)). Wait, let's compute: at t=0, P is at (5,2). At t=1, P moves to (5,1). So if I move W to (5,2) at t=1, I arrive at (5,2) at t=1, but P is now at (5,1), so I'm safe. Then I can go N to (5,3), (5,4), (5,5), (5,6), (5,7), (5,8), (5,9). But I also need to pick up parcel 2 at (5,4). I can do that on the way. Let's plan: from (9,2), go W to (5,2) (t=1), then N to (5,9) (t=7). But I need to check guards along the way. At t=1, P is at (5,1), Q is at (6,2). At t=2, P moves to (6,1), Q moves to (5,2). So if I'm at (5,2) at t=1, and then move N to (5,3) at t=2, I'm safe (Q is at (5,2)). Then at t=3, P moves to (6,2), Q moves to (6,2). I can continue N. At t=4, P moves to (7,2), Q moves to (5,2). I can continue N. At t=5, P moves to (6,2), Q moves to (6,2). I can continue N. At t=6, P moves to (6,1), Q moves to (5,2). I can continue N. At t=7, P moves to (5,1), Q moves to (6,2). I arrive at (5,9) at t=7. Then I can go to (8,3) for delivery. From (5,9), I can go E to (6,9) (gate A is open at t=8? Let's check: gate A open when t mod 4 in {1,2}. At t=8, 8 mod 4 = 0, so closed. So I can't go through gate A at t=8. I need to go around. Maybe go E to (6,9) at t=8 (but gate closed, bump), so I stay at (5,9). Then at t=9, gate A is open (9 mod 4 = 1), so I can go E to (6,9). Then from (6,9), I can go S to (6,8), (6,7), (6,6), (6,5), (6,4), (6,3), (6,2), (6,1), then E to (7,3), (8,3). But I need to check guards. Alternatively, I can go from (5,9) to (5,8), (5,7), etc., but that's longer. Let's try a different route: from (5,9), go E to (6,9) at t=8 (bump, stay at (5,9)), then at t=9 go E to (6,9), then S to (6,8) at t=10, (6,7) at t=11, (6,6) at t=12, (6,5) at t=13, (6,4) at t=14, (6,3) at t=15, (6,2) at t=16, (6,1) at t=17, then E to (7,3) at t=18, (8,3) at t=19. But I also need to pick up parcel 2 at (5,4). I can do that on the way from (9,2) to (5,9). When I go N from (5,2) to (5,9), I pass (5,4) at t=4 (since (5,2) at t=1, (5,3) at t=2, (5,4) at t=3, (5,5) at t=4, etc.). Wait, let's recalculate: from (9,2) to (5,2): t=0 at (9,2), t=1 at (5,2) (after 4 W moves? No, from (9,2) to (5,2) is 4 steps W: (9,2)->(8,2)->(7,2)->(6,2)->(5,2). So t=1 at (8,2), t=2 at (7,2), t=3 at (6,2), t=4 at (5,2). Then from (5,2) to (5,9): t=5 at (5,3), t=6 at (5,4), t=7 at (5,5), t=8 at (5,6), t=9 at (5,7), t=10 at (5,8), t=11 at (5,9). So I pick up parcel 2 at (5,4) at t=6, and parcel 1 at (5,9) at t=11. Then I need to deliver parcel 1 to (8,3) and parcel 2 to (5,5). Parcel 2's delivery is at (5,5), which I pass at t=7. So I can deliver parcel 2 at t=7. Then I need to deliver parcel 1 to (8,3). From (5,9), I can go to (8,3). Let's find a path. (5,9) to (8,3): I can go E to (6,9), then S to (6,3), then E to (8,3). But gate A at (6,9) is open at t=12? Let's check: t=12 mod 4 = 0, closed. t=13 mod 4 = 1, open. So I can go E at t=13. Then from (6,9) to (6,3): S to (6,8) at t=14, (6,7) at t=15, (6,6) at t=16, (6,5) at t=17, (6,4) at t=18, (6,3) at t=19. Then E to (7,3) at t=20, (8,3) at t=21. So total time: t=0 to t=21. That's within 40. But I need to check guards along the way. At t=6, I'm at (5,4) picking up parcel 2. At t=7, I'm at (5,5) delivering parcel 2. At t=11, I'm at (5,9) picking up parcel 1. Then at t=13, I go E to (6,9). At t=13, guard P is at (6,2) (since t=13 mod 8 = 5, P at (6,2)), guard Q is at (6,2) (t=13 mod 4 = 1, Q at (6,2)). So (6,9) is safe. Then S to (6,8) at t=14: P at (7,2), Q at (5,2). Safe. Continue S to (6,3) at t=19: P at (6,1) (t=19 mod 8 = 3, P at (6,2)? Wait, t=19 mod 8 = 3, P at (6,2). Q at t=19 mod 4 = 3, Q at (6,2). So (6,3) is safe. Then E to (7,3) at t=20: P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe. Then E to (8,3) at t=21: P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe. So this plan works. But I need to make sure I don't get caught. Let's verify each step:

t=0: start at (9,2).
t=1: move W to (8,2). P at (5,1), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=2: move W to (7,2). P at (6,1), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=3: move W to (6,2). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Oh, I'm at (6,2) and both guards are at (6,2). So I get caught at t=3. That's bad. I need to avoid (6,2). So I should not go through (6,2). Instead, from (9,2), I can go N to (9,3), then W to (5,3), then N to (5,9). Let's try that.

From (9,2), go N to (9,3) at t=1. Then W to (8,3) at t=2, (7,3) at t=3, (6,3) at t=4, (5,3) at t=5. Then N to (5,4) at t=6, (5,5) at t=7, (5,6) at t=8, (5,7) at t=9, (5,8) at t=10, (5,9) at t=11. Now check guards:

t=1: at (9,3). P at (5,1), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=2: at (8,3). P at (6,1), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=3: at (7,3). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=4: at (6,3). P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=5: at (5,3). P at (7,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=6: at (5,4). P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe. Pick up parcel 2.
t=7: at (5,5). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe. Deliver parcel 2.
t=8: at (5,6). P at (7,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=9: at (5,7). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=10: at (5,8). P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=11: at (5,9). P at (7,2), Q at (6,2). Safe. Pick up parcel 1.

Now from (5,9), go to (8,3). I can go E to (6,9) at t=12. But gate A at (6,9) is open when t mod 4 in {1,2}. t=12 mod 4 = 0, closed. So I can't go E at t=12. I need to wait or go another way. I can go S to (6,9) is not possible because gate closed. Instead, I can go E to (6,9) at t=13 (when gate open). But to get to (6,9), I need to be at (5,9) and move E. At t=12, I'm at (5,9). I can WAIT at t=12, then at t=13 move E to (6,9). Then S to (6,8) at t=14, (6,7) at t=15, (6,6) at t=16, (6,5) at t=17, (6,4) at t=18, (6,3) at t=19. Then E to (7,3) at t=20, (8,3) at t=21. Check guards:

t=12: at (5,9). P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=13: move E to (6,9). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=14: move S to (6,8). P at (7,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=15: move S to (6,7). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=16: move S to (6,6). P at (7,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=17: move S to (6,5). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=18: move S to (6,4). P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe.
t=19: move S to (6,3). P at (7,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=20: move E to (7,3). P at (6,2), Q at (6,2). Safe.
t=21: move E to (8,3). P at (6,2), Q at (5,2). Safe. Deliver parcel 1.

So total time 21 ticks. Now I need to deliver parcel 2 to (5,5), which I did at t=7. So both parcels delivered. But I need to make sure I don't get caught. At t=3, I was at (7,3), which is safe. At t=15, I was at (6,7), safe. At t=19, I was at (6,3), safe. At t=21, I deliver parcel 1. So this plan works.

Now, I need to output the first plan of 5 moves. From (9,2), the first 5 moves should be: N, W, W, W, N. Let's see: t=0 at (9,2). t=1: N to (9,3). t=2: W to (8,3). t=3: W to (7,3). t=4: W to (6,3). t=5: N to (5,3). So the first plan is "N W W W N".

POSITION: (5,3)
PLAN: N W W W N
